steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what was surprising about the mapping of G47's magnetic field. The passage states: "Surprisingly, the map revealed a magnetic field with no clear pattern or direction." The researchers had expected a field similar to the uniform fields of other galactic bones. The answer must reflect that unexpected lack of uniformity.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It implied that previous mappings of the magnetic field were inaccurate.: the passage never says earlier mappings existed or were wrong — only that the result was unexpected. ✗
  • Choice B — It produced magnetic field measurements similar to those for other galactic bones.: the opposite of what happened — the field was unlike the uniform fields seen in other galactic bones. ✗
  • Choice C — It revealed a magnetic field that wasn't uniform.: directly matches "no clear pattern or direction," which is exactly what surprised the researchers who expected uniformity. ✓
  • Choice D — It showed a weaker magnetic field than expected.: field strength is never mentioned in the passage; the surprise was about pattern and direction, not intensity. ✗
That matches Choice C — It revealed a magnetic field that wasn't uniform..
